Well
you always hear words like retal team, poison team, Gelot team, Tawil team

but in reality 
the only difference is the core units
most supports work with most team (still confine to their own realm)

so a retal team will have a core for them
for an example Caecus
so you will look at his skills and determine what kind of supports he needs

Caecus retal scales with tentacle dmg
so someone like Tulu or Miryam is good with him

Nautila is a shield and retal enabler
which is what Caecus like
Pandia can uses all the retal from Caecus to boost her exalt damage
Did a scuffed Caros retaliate team for fun. And by that I mean it was painful, but at least I got to use Agrippa whom I never used before

ofc
universal support like Ham (can reduce cost, inspiration and double Caecus retal output), Ryker (money farming for better relics and crit buff, card draw), Karen (your old school exalt bot)
so yeah

just pick someone as your core
and build your team around them

believe in poison Caro
by buffing Agrippa with a lot of atk buff and Ham
I did Alva, Faint, Thais, Agrippa
Scaling that alert for that shielding and retaliation

Because Agrippa's rouse givees her 50% more poison  if her 2 cost did 1000 damage, it applies 1500 poison instead
Plus any poison bonuses from wheels/covenants

||Just finished chapter 8 and I dont understand anything.
1 Juliette wanted to open the door to a cosmic God. For what reason? to kill everybody? to kill the sinners? who are the sinners? She got second thoughts at the end. why? too much killing for her taste (a woman who treat everybody  as "dog")?
2. In theory, Hameln crossed both Juliette and the Keeper. why? He says in some point that the coming of the god is inevitable... but after sealing the door and defeating the final boss and rescue Ramona and Tawil, he thinks the've won and they've stopped the ritual. 1. Religious cult, standard stuff in lore. God will rebirth the world, etc. Common theme in Lovecraft based works. 2. Tawil and Ramona are kinda the same person. Ramona was created from Tawil's wish to observe the world. Though developed differently, they need all of Tawil, including Ramona.

https://lovecraft.fandom.com/wiki/Yog-Sothoth Also, most of the story was based on love craft outer gods. Mostly Yog-Sothoth

embeds.title: Yog-Sothoth
embeds.description: Yog-Sothoth is a fictional cosmic entity and Outer God created by H. P. Lovecraft. He is a core part of the Cthulhu Mythos (which Lovecraft would refer to facetiously as "Yog-Sothothery"). Born of...

content: Uvhash is also named after a similar Lovecraft character
Also  Tawil is based after https://lovecraft.fandom.com/wiki/%27Umr_At-Tawil which serves a similar role

embeds.title: 'Umr At-Tawil
embeds.description: 'Umr At-Tawil (عمر الطويل) is an entity who stands beside the Ultimate Gate, meeting any who traverse the first Gate via the Silver Key. He appears as a heavily cloaked, vaguely humanoid figure...

content: But genderbent, as holding door for Yog Sothoth
Silver Key, gates, etc all copied or inspired by Lovecraft, with a few modern twists
